description: The MaterialApplyPolicy component allows for controlling whether a Material Tool is able to apply a material to the slot hierarchy tagged with this component onto its SkinnedMeshRenderer/MeshRenderer components.

fields.CanApply: Setting this to false will prevent the Material Tool from applying materials to any SkinnedMeshRenderer/MeshRenderer components in the slot or child slots.

notes.title: <translate> Usage</translate>
notes.text: When this component is added to a Slot with a SkinnedMeshRenderer or MeshRenderer attached, it allows one to limit the effects of the Material Tool.

notes.title: <translate> Examples</translate>
notes.text:
On an avatar, you can try adding this component to the root of the avatar, making sure the avatar has a ObjectRoot, and set ``CanApply`` to false. Doing this will ensure that the avatar's material cannot be changed by the Material Tool.

Adding this component to the Slot of a MeshRender and setting ``CanApply`` to true can allow one to explicitly change the material in specific places on small objects.
